jokes.text: General rule of thumb... if you see nose hair, it's too close. Aha!
jokes.setup: Warning about camera getting too close on Larry
jokes.punchline: The specific, gross visual indicator of 'too close'
jokes.explanation: Sharp, specific observational humor about TV production. The nose hair detail is perfectly chosen - gross but relatable.

dialogue.line: General rule of thumb... if you see nose hair, it's too close. Aha!

jokes.text: Checks for Larry, birds for Hank... aw, throw that one away.
jokes.punchline: Hank gets such bad fan mail it goes straight to trash
jokes.explanation: Efficient character comedy that establishes Hank's sad-sack status through a throwaway detail.

dialogue.line: Checks for Larry, birds for Hank... aw, throw that one away.

jokes.text: As much as I'd love Larry to ask her about her gorgeous new tits, I feel we covered that front quite well when miss butler was here in may.
jokes.setup: Reviewing interview questions for Brett Butler
jokes.punchline: 'covered that front' - double entendre about breasts/topics
jokes.explanation: Competent wordplay but relies on fairly obvious double meaning.

dialogue.line: As much as I'd love Larry to ask her about her gorgeous new tits, I feel we covered that front quite well when miss butler was here in may.

jokes.text: You have to forgive me. I've got my period.
jokes.punchline: Oversharing intimate detail as excuse
jokes.explanation: Basic workplace oversharing humor - functional but predictable.

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: You have to forgive me. I've got my period.

jokes.text: You know, you used that excuse 10 weeks ago, memory serves. That means you're not due to start your cycle until the 22nd or 23rd.
jokes.setup: Paula claims to have her period
jokes.punchline: Artie has been tracking her menstrual cycle
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Artie's obsessive, inappropriate knowledge reveals his creepy attention to detail. Perfect escalation.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: You know, you used that excuse 10 weeks ago, memory serves. That means you're not due to start your cycle until the 22nd or 23rd.

jokes.text: Well, how do you know I wasn't lying the last time?
jokes.setup: Artie caught her in menstrual cycle inconsistency
jokes.punchline: Maybe she was lying then too
jokes.explanation: Smart comeback that extends the joke and shows Paula fighting back.

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: Well, how do you know I wasn't lying the last time?

jokes.text: You were a bit puffy.
jokes.punchline: He noticed physical bloating symptoms
jokes.explanation: Perfectly crafted follow-up that makes Artie even creepier. The economy is perfect - just three words.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: You were a bit puffy.

jokes.text: Oh, you're good. You're not the first woman who's told me that.
jokes.punchline: Artie brags about his menstrual tracking skills with other women
jokes.explanation: Escalates the creepiness further - Artie's proud of this horrible skill.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Oh, you're good. You're not the first woman who's told me that.

jokes.text: Do you like history, Phil? What does that mean? Because if Larry doesn't want to wear the hat, that's what it becomes, dig?
jokes.setup: Phil complains about Larry not wearing costume hat
jokes.punchline: Artie's pseudo-philosophical response about sketch becoming 'history'
jokes.explanation: Shows Artie's pretentious way of deflecting problems with vague wisdom.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Do you like history, Phil? What does that mean? Because if Larry doesn't want to wear the hat, that's what it becomes, dig?

jokes.text: He is supposed to be the gorton's fisherman. Without the hat, he's just a guy in a yellow slicker.
jokes.punchline: How specific the costume requirement is for the parody to work
jokes.explanation: Solid observational humor about how specific visual cues work in comedy.

dialogue.speaker: Phil
dialogue.line: He is supposed to be the gorton's fisherman. Without the hat, he's just a guy in a yellow slicker.

jokes.text: What? Makes me laugh. What makes you laugh, the sketch? No. The word 'slicker' makes me chuckle.
jokes.setup: Phil frustrated about the sketch dying
jokes.punchline: Artie fixated on finding the word 'slicker' funny
jokes.explanation: Perfect Artie moment - his mind works differently, finding humor in random words while missing the real problem.

dialogue.speaker: Phil
dialogue.line: What? Makes me laugh.

dialogue.speaker: Phil
dialogue.line: What makes you laugh, the sketch?

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: No. The word 'slicker' makes me chuckle.

jokes.text: Say it again. Slicker. Use it in a sentence. I like my slicker. Ha ha.
jokes.punchline: He makes Phil repeat it and use it in a sentence like a vocabulary lesson
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - Artie treats Phil like a child while completely ignoring the work crisis.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Say it again. Slicker. Use it in a sentence.

dialogue.speaker: Phil
dialogue.line: I like my slicker.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Ha ha.

jokes.text: Artie, I'm gonna kick somebody's ass, I swear.
jokes.setup: Artie's word fixation while Phil has real problems
jokes.punchline: Phil's exasperated threat
jokes.explanation: Standard frustrated reaction but works as payoff to Artie's absurd behavior.

dialogue.speaker: Phil
dialogue.line: Artie, I'm gonna kick somebody's ass, I swear.

jokes.text: Profanity, beverly. I love it.
jokes.punchline: Artie's appreciation for her profanity
jokes.explanation: Shows Artie's odd values - he appreciates colorful language over actual problems.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Profanity, beverly. I love it.

jokes.text: that wendy bitch in charge of the audience... she's got her way in the hell up there where we put the retards.
jokes.setup: Beverly's mother visiting for first time
jokes.punchline: She's been seated in the section for 'retards'
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about show business treating audience poorly, though relies on outdated terminology.

dialogue.speaker: Beverly
dialogue.line: that wendy bitch in charge of the audience... she's got her way in the hell up there where we put the retards.

jokes.text: We call them 'mentally challenged' now, beverly.
jokes.punchline: Artie's PC correction while missing the larger point
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Artie focuses on language while ignoring that they segregate the audience.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: We call them 'mentally challenged' now, beverly.

jokes.text: Don't twist your skivvies in a knot, sugar.
jokes.setup: Beverly upset about her mother's treatment
jokes.punchline: Artie's folksy dismissal
jokes.explanation: Typical Artie-ism - colorful expression while being dismissive of real concerns.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Don't twist your skivvies in a knot, sugar.

jokes.text: Having fun showing her around? Artie, the woman is my mother.
jokes.setup: Artie asks if Beverly's having fun with her mother's visit
jokes.punchline: Beverly's flat response emphasizing the burden
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - the weight of family obligation in four words.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Having fun showing her around?

dialogue.speaker: Beverly
dialogue.line: Artie, the woman is my mother.

jokes.text: Is there blood? Everywhere.
jokes.punchline: Artie's dramatic assumption about blood being everywhere
jokes.explanation: Great economy and escalation - Artie immediately jumps to the most dramatic scenario.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Is there blood? Everywhere.

jokes.text: What, can't find your way upstream to spawn?
jokes.punchline: Artie treats him like an actual fish
jokes.explanation: Clever visual comedy - Artie commits to the fish costume bit perfectly.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: What, can't find your way upstream to spawn?

jokes.text: someone has been touching my karaoke machine.
jokes.punchline: It's about someone using his karaoke machine
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hank - treating a minor inconvenience as a major crisis.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: someone has been touching my karaoke machine.

jokes.text: Oh, God, no!
jokes.punchline: Artie's mock horror at this 'tragedy'
jokes.explanation: Perfect sarcastic reaction to Hank's overblown concern.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Oh, God, no!

jokes.text: Really? No.
jokes.setup: Hank suggests using karaoke machine on show
jokes.punchline: Artie's instant flat rejection
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing - lets Hank get excited then crushes it immediately.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Really?

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: No.

jokes.text: I walk in the morning, the red light is on, and this baby is just warm to the touch
jokes.setup: Describing evidence of karaoke machine use
jokes.punchline: The intimate way he describes touching the machine
jokes.explanation: Hank's creepy relationship with objects - talks about the machine like a lover.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: I walk in the morning, the red light is on, and this baby is just warm to the touch

jokes.text: I know how your people enjoy the karaoke.
jokes.setup: Asking Bri if he used the machine
jokes.punchline: Racist assumption about Asian people and karaoke
jokes.explanation: Shows Hank's casual racism - perfectly in character but relies on stereotype.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: I know how your people enjoy the karaoke.

jokes.text: Now if you'll excuse me, I must go call my parents and tell them I'm asian.
jokes.punchline: Bri's sarcastic response about discovering his race
jokes.explanation: Brilliant comeback that skewers Hank's racism with perfect sarcasm.

dialogue.speaker: Bri
dialogue.line: Now if you'll excuse me, I must go call my parents and tell them I'm asian.

jokes.text: I miss darlene. And not just because of, you know, the, uh, the sweaters.
jokes.setup: Hank expressing nostalgia for former employee
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's about her physical attributes
jokes.explanation: Typical Hank inappropriateness - can't separate professional from personal feelings.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: I miss darlene. And not just because of, you know, the, uh, the sweaters.

jokes.text: You know, that was the least graceful entrance we've ever had on the show.
jokes.punchline: Larry immediately criticizes her entrance on air
jokes.explanation: Shows Larry's neurotic need to comment on show mechanics during the show.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: You know, that was the least graceful entrance we've ever had on the show.

jokes.text: I would rather sometimes be allowed to be the man.
jokes.setup: Discussion about who leads their interactions
jokes.punchline: Larry's insecure plea to be dominant
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry insecurity - even needs to negotiate masculinity on air.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I would rather sometimes be allowed to be the man.

jokes.text: ♪ do-do-do-do-do-do-do ♪
jokes.punchline: Sandra turns it into an elaborate musical riff
jokes.explanation: Sandra's manic energy turning a simple word into performance art.

dialogue.speaker: Sandra
dialogue.line: ♪ do-do-do-do-do-do-do ♪

jokes.text: I got into bed with them. They had plenty of room.
jokes.setup: Visited Lisa Marie and Michael Jackson's house
jokes.punchline: Implies their marriage isn't physical
jokes.explanation: Sharp celebrity gossip humor with good economy and dark implication.

dialogue.speaker: Sandra
dialogue.line: I got into bed with them. They had plenty of room.

jokes.text: They're arguing, or they're burying stuff in the backyard?
jokes.setup: Sandra says they go at it like cats and dogs
jokes.punchline: Larry's dark interpretation about burying things
jokes.explanation: Clever escalation of animal metaphor with dark Michael Jackson implications.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: They're arguing, or they're burying stuff in the backyard?

jokes.text: All at once, they're just coming at me 100,000 miles a minute. Who is? Everybody in the business.
jokes.punchline: Absurd speed measurement for business opportunities
jokes.explanation: Sandra's manic hyperbole - functional but not exceptional.

dialogue.speaker: Sandra
dialogue.line: All at once, they're just coming at me 100,000 miles a minute. Who is? Everybody in the business.

jokes.text: Not a damn thing. I've been fired off everything. No, you haven't. I'm kidding.
jokes.punchline: Immediately reveals the opposite then says she's kidding
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing Sandra's unpredictable interview style.

dialogue.speaker: Sandra
dialogue.line: Not a damn thing. I've been fired off everything.
No, you haven't.

dialogue.speaker: Sandra
dialogue.line: I'm kidding.

jokes.text: I have an erection. Funny, so do I.
jokes.punchline: Sandra matches his confession
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation and economy - Sandra one-ups Larry's inappropriate confession perfectly.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I have an erection.

dialogue.speaker: Sandra
dialogue.line: Funny, so do I.

jokes.text: Hey, 2 erections and nowhere to go.
jokes.punchline: Sandra's summary of their situation
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up line that caps the absurd moment with economy.

dialogue.speaker: Sandra
dialogue.line: Hey, 2 erections and nowhere to go.

jokes.text: Hey, thanks for being a soldier about this, rye bread.
jokes.setup: Larry bumping Ryan O'Neal from show
jokes.punchline: Using condescending nickname while screwing him over
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry character moment - being fake-friendly while delivering bad news.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Hey, thanks for being a soldier about this, rye bread.

jokes.text: Make you feel better to hit me? Go ahead. Shut up. You know you want to. I'm not hitting anymore.
jokes.punchline: Artie goads him to violence then Ryan reveals he's reformed
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - shows Hollywood's violent reputation and attempts at reform.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Make you feel better to hit me? Go ahead. Shut up. You know you want to.

dialogue.speaker: Ryan
dialogue.line: I'm not hitting anymore.

jokes.text: And the next time you need somebody to pick you up after one of your d.u.I. S, don't call me.
jokes.punchline: Reveals Artie's drinking problems and their friendship history
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that reveals character backstory and their complicated friendship.

dialogue.speaker: Ryan
dialogue.line: And the next time you need somebody to pick you up after one of your d.u.I. S, don't call me.

jokes.text: And don't call me rye bread. It's what farrah calls me.
jokes.punchline: The nickname is reserved for Farrah Fawcett
jokes.explanation: Perfect final dig - shows the intimacy levels in his relationships.

dialogue.speaker: Ryan
dialogue.line: And don't call me rye bread. It's what farrah calls me.

jokes.text: Speaking of my ass, if it gets any bigger, it's gonna need a publicist. I'm not kidding. It's gonna have a career of its own soon.
jokes.punchline: Escalates to his ass needing representation
jokes.explanation: Good self-deprecating humor with Hollywood business angle escalation.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Speaking of my ass, if it gets any bigger, it's gonna need a publicist. I'm not kidding. It's gonna have a career of its own soon.

jokes.text: She's probably screwing hans brinker. She's making his skates into earrings as we speak.
jokes.punchline: Absurd jealousy scenario with Dutch cultural reference
jokes.explanation: Creative jealousy fantasy with good cultural specificity and absurd detail.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: She's probably screwing hans brinker. She's making his skates into earrings as we speak.

jokes.text: That's not my problem now, is it, sweetie?
jokes.setup: Larry points out Artie upset Ryan
jokes.punchline: Artie's dismissive response with condescending endearment
jokes.explanation: Shows Artie's ability to deflect responsibility with fake charm.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: That's not my problem now, is it, sweetie?

jokes.text: I'm not sending you one thin gilder.
jokes.setup: Fighting with Dutch ex-wife about money
jokes.punchline: Uses Dutch currency in his insult
jokes.explanation: Clever use of appropriate currency in the insult shows Artie's attention to detail even when angry.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: I'm not sending you one thin gilder.

jokes.text: go to rotterdam, then you go to amsterdam, then you go down the road to my city. That's right. It's a little place called I don't give a damn.
jokes.punchline: Leads to fictional city 'I don't give a damn'
jokes.explanation: Excellent build-up through real Dutch cities to the punchline destination. Good structure and quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: go to rotterdam, then you go to amsterdam, then you go down the road to my city. That's right. It's a little place called I don't give a damn.

jokes.text: Of course. You didn't really expect to find him here.
jokes.setup: Larry looking for Artie who left
jokes.punchline: Beverly's matter-of-fact observation about avoidance
jokes.explanation: Beverly's dry wisdom about office dynamics and conflict avoidance.

dialogue.speaker: Beverly
dialogue.line: Of course. You didn't really expect to find him here.

jokes.text: He's lucky he's not in that chair, the fucking asshole. Fuck him! Fuck me for taking this shit for so long!
jokes.punchline: Escalates to cursing himself for enabling it
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Larry's rare moment of genuine anger and self-awareness.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: He's lucky he's not in that chair, the fucking asshole. Fuck him! Fuck me for taking this shit for so long!

jokes.text: Beverly? I'm coming, ma!
jokes.punchline: Beverly has to immediately switch to sweet daughter mode
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to earlier setup about her mother's visit, showing the code-switching required in professional settings.

dialogue.speaker: Beverly
dialogue.line: I'm coming, ma!

jokes.text: There's those letters I hate. There they are. I hate those fucking letters.
jokes.punchline: His irrational hatred of letters (presumably show logo)
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hank neurosis - finding something arbitrary to hate about his workplace.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: There's those letters I hate. There they are. I hate those fucking letters.

jokes.text: And now, because he's a big, phony bastard, Larry Sanders!
jokes.punchline: Insults Larry while introducing him
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of the traditional show intro - Hank's passive-aggressive revenge through his professional duties.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: And now, because he's a big, phony bastard, Larry Sanders!

jokes.text: Yeah, I recognize that toluca lake accent.
jokes.setup: Nicolae says he's from Toluca Lake, California
jokes.punchline: Artie's sarcastic response about the non-existent accent
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic comeback that highlights Nicolae's obvious attempt to hide his origin.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Yeah, I recognize that toluca lake accent.

jokes.text: No, dipshit. I mean recently, before toluca lake.
jokes.punchline: Artie's blunt clarification with insult
jokes.explanation: Shows Artie's direct, sometimes crude way of cutting through BS.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: No, dipshit. I mean recently, before toluca lake.

jokes.text: You just told me that you watch the show a lot. Yes, but is no Larry.
jokes.setup: Nicolae claims to watch the show regularly
jokes.punchline: He doesn't know who Larry Sanders is
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor - watches a show but doesn't know the main character.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: You just told me that you watch the show a lot.

dialogue.speaker: Nicolae
dialogue.line: Yes, but is no Larry.

jokes.text: Hank. Hank is very good. Ha! You know Hank? Hey now!
jokes.setup: Nicolae knows Hank but not Larry
jokes.punchline: He can even do Hank's catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic reversal - the janitor knows the sidekick better than the star.

dialogue.speaker: Nicolae
dialogue.line: Hank. Hank is very good. Ha! You know Hank? Hey now!

jokes.text: You know why we get along, nic? 'Cause we're brothers. We're the unsung heroes. I clean up shit all day, you clean up shit all night.
jokes.punchline: They both clean up different kinds of 'shit'
jokes.explanation: Excellent metaphor that connects Artie's professional role with Nicolae's literal job. Shows Artie's self-awareness about his position.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: You know why we get along, nic? 'Cause we're brothers. We're the unsung heroes. I clean up shit all day, you clean up shit all night.

jokes.text: When you die, you'll go to heaven, you say hello to God, and when God says hello to you, this is what you'll smell on his breath.
jokes.setup: Describing the quality of scotch whiskey
jokes.punchline: God drinks this scotch
jokes.explanation: Creative way to describe premium alcohol quality with religious imagery.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: When you die, you'll go to heaven, you say hello to God, and when God says hello to you, this is what you'll smell on his breath.

jokes.text: You're lucky, nic, 'cause the shit I clean up talks back.
jokes.setup: Earlier metaphor about cleaning up shit
jokes.punchline: Artie's shit talks back (the people he manages)
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that extends the metaphor. Excellent economy and insight into Artie's job frustrations.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: You're lucky, nic, 'cause the shit I clean up talks back.

jokes.text: I'm gonna resist the urge to, uh, express my anger by using profanity, and I hope you appreciate that, you... scum-sucking asshole!
jokes.setup: Artie promises not to use profanity in his message
jokes.punchline: Immediately breaks that promise
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection - sets up expectation of restraint then immediately violates it.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: I'm gonna resist the urge to, uh, express my anger by using profanity, and I hope you appreciate that, you... scum-sucking asshole!

jokes.text: I wish you great success with the show. And personally, I have no ill will toward you... but hope your balls fall off.
jokes.setup: Artie trying to sound professional and magnanimous
jokes.punchline: Ends with curse about Larry's anatomy
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of the misdirection pattern - professional tone undercut by crude wishes.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: I wish you great success with the show. And personally, I have no ill will toward you... but hope your balls fall off.

jokes.text: but does it crack? Does it? No!
jokes.setup: Artie comparing himself to building beams
jokes.punchline: His emphatic denial that he cracks under pressure
jokes.explanation: Shows Artie's drunk philosophical state and self-image as indispensable.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: but does it crack? Does it? No!

jokes.text: Because all the pressure in this building, all the stress goes right into that, but does it crack? Does it? / No! / Aw, say, you bet your ass. Damn right it doesn't.
jokes.setup: Artie's metaphor about being structural support
jokes.punchline: Getting Nicolae to participate in his drunk self-aggrandizement
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of the structural metaphor with audience participation element making it more pathetic and funny.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Because all the pressure in this building, all the stress goes right into that, but does it crack? Does it?

dialogue.speaker: Nicolae
dialogue.line: No!

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Aw, say, you bet your ass. Damn right it doesn't.

jokes.text: 'Cause all people remember is the night the chimp grabbed Larry's balls.
jokes.setup: Artie lamenting what his career amounts to
jokes.punchline: References a specific incident from show history
jokes.explanation: Great callback to show lore that illustrates Artie's point about what people remember from TV.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: 'Cause all people remember is the night the chimp grabbed Larry's balls.

jokes.text: That's the way it adds up... just a monkey and some balls.
jokes.punchline: Reduces his life's work to this crude summary
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy in summarizing a lifetime of work. Dark and quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: That's the way it adds up... just a monkey and some balls.

jokes.text: Here. Iooky here. I want you to take this. Go on, take it. / No. / Fuck your pride. Keep the clip, too. / Too very many.

jokes.explanation: Shows Artie's generosity and Nicolae's dignity. The language barrier humor is gentle.

dialogue.speaker: Nicolae
dialogue.line: No.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Fuck your pride. Keep the clip, too.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: You're still the fucking janitor, aren't you?

jokes.text: I hope your balls falling off!

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: He's smiling like that because his refund check from the Betty ford clinic just came in the mail today.

jokes.text: Prick.
jokes.punchline: Artie's simple, defeated response
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - one word that says everything about Artie's defeat and recognition of Larry's skill.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Prick.

jokes.text: Everything is Dan-Dan-dandy.
jokes.setup: Hank reporting on karaoke machine situation
jokes.punchline: His elaborate way of saying 'dandy'
jokes.explanation: Typical Hank verbal flourish - unnecessarily elaborate way of expressing simple concepts.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Everything is Dan-Dan-dandy.

jokes.text: Warm, moist, muffin-y goodness. Courtney Cox? Oh, close. A muffin.
jokes.setup: Beverly describing something to make Larry smile
jokes.punchline: Larry's sexual interpretation vs. innocent reality
jokes.explanation: Shows Larry's mind in the gutter - innocent words trigger sexual associations.

dialogue.speaker: Beverly
dialogue.line: Warm, moist, muffin-y goodness. Courtney Cox? Oh, close. A muffin.

jokes.text: He's got this friend nicolae. Whew. What a mouth on that one.
jokes.punchline: Blames Nicolae for the profanity
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Beverly covering for Artie by blaming the innocent foreigner.

dialogue.speaker: Beverly
dialogue.line: He's got this friend nicolae. Whew. What a mouth on that one.
